steps.title: Identify the norm framing givers' judgment
steps.content:
The passage establishes that “time spent obtaining a gift” signals thoughtfulness.

This means givers would view digital cards—quick and easy to purchase online—as requiring low effort and therefore low thoughtfulness.

Yet the finding shows recipients actually prefer digital for usage, revealing a gap between what givers assume and what recipients actually value.

steps.title: Determine what givers wrongly assume
steps.content:
Givers perceive digital cards as requiring low effort to obtain and thus wrongly assume recipients will appreciate them less—this is what D states.
  • A confuses effort to use (not obtain)
  • B contradicts the stated usage preference
  • C reverses the social acceptability finding
.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
